profiles.name: Cambridge - PhD Thesis (APA 7)
profiles.author: TeXisStudio Community
profiles.description: University of Cambridge PhD thesis for social sciences, psychology, and education. Follows Cambridge Graduate Office format guidelines: A4, 40mm left margin (binding), double-spacing, 12pt, APA 7 bibliography via Biber. Word limit: 80,000 words.

changes: Corrected left margin to 40mm (Cambridge binding requirement)
Added Declaration section (required by Cambridge Graduate Office)
Added verification block with official source URLs
Added comprehensive section guidance for each chapter
Added word limit (80,000) to Introduction guidance
Added Abstract 300-word limit and ProQuest submission note

profiles.verified_by: TeXisStudio CI — automated LaTeX compilation + postflight (xelatex + biber)

citation_styles.name: APA 7
citation_styles.description: The most widely used citation style globally. Required by most psychology, education, and social science programs. Seventh edition (2020) introduced DOI as hyperlink, up to 20 authors, and cleaner formatting rules.
citation_styles.notes: Default style for most Latin American universities. Nearly universal at bachelor/master level in Mexico.

citation_styles.name: Vancouver
citation_styles.organization: International Committee of Medical Journal Editors
citation_styles.description: Numeric citation style mandatory for health sciences and medicine worldwide. References are numbered in order of appearance. Standard for most clinical and biomedical journals indexed in MEDLINE/PubMed.
citation_styles.notes: Required for UNAM medicine, IPN health programs, and most Latin American medical schools.

citation_styles.name: IEEE
citation_styles.organization: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
citation_styles.description: Numeric style standard for engineering, computer science, and technology disciplines. References are numbered by order of citation. Widely adopted at MIT, Stanford, TU Delft, TU Munich, IIT Bombay, NUS, and virtually all engineering schools globally.
citation_styles.notes: De facto standard for engineering theses worldwide.

citation_styles.name: Chicago 17 (Notes)
citation_styles.full_name: Chicago Manual of Style, 17th Edition — Notes-Bibliography
citation_styles.description: Notes-Bibliography system used in humanities disciplines. Citations appear as footnotes or endnotes; a full bibliography is included at the end. Preferred at Harvard, UChicago, and many UK and US humanities departments.
citation_styles.notes: Dominant in history and literature programs. Oxford and Cambridge humanities also accept this style.

citation_styles.name: Chicago 17 (Author-Date)
citation_styles.full_name: Chicago Manual of Style, 17th Edition — Author-Date
citation_styles.description: Author-Date variant of Chicago, preferred in social sciences and economics. In-text citations use (Author Year) format. Required at UChicago social science programs and common in economics globally.
citation_styles.notes: Often used alongside Notes-Bibliography at the same institution, depending on department.

citation_styles.name: MLA 9
citation_styles.description: Standard for language, literature, and cultural studies in North America. Uses parenthetical author-page citations. Ninth edition (2021) simplified formatting rules and introduced more flexible source types.
citation_styles.notes: Required for literature and language programs at Harvard, Yale, and most US liberal arts colleges.

citation_styles.name: Harvard
citation_styles.organization: No single governing body — widely adopted standard
regions_primary: parts of Europe and Latin America
citation_styles.description: Author-date style similar to APA, widely used in UK and Australian universities. Common in business, management, and social science programs outside the USA. No single authoritative manual — institutions often define their own variant.
citation_styles.notes: Often preferred over APA at European MBAs and UK social science programs.

citation_styles.name: MHRA
citation_styles.full_name: Modern Humanities Research Association Style Guide, 3rd Edition
citation_styles.description: UK humanities standard similar to Chicago Notes-Bibliography. Widely required at Oxford, Cambridge, UCL, and other Russell Group universities for dissertations in history, literature, and arts.
citation_styles.notes: Oxford's default style for many humanities departments. Slightly different from Chicago Notes in formatting.

citation_styles.name: ABNT
citation_styles.full_name: ABNT NBR 6023:2018 — Associacao Brasileira de Normas Tecnicas
regions_primary: Brazil — mandatory for all Brazilian university theses
citation_styles.description: Mandatory standard for all academic works submitted to Brazilian universities. NBR 6023:2018 governs bibliographic references; NBR 10520:2002 governs in-text citations. Required at USP, UNICAMP, UFRJ, and all federal and state universities.
citation_styles.notes: Not optional in Brazil — any thesis submitted to a Brazilian institution must use ABNT.

citation_styles.name: GB/T 7714
citation_styles.full_name: GB/T 7714-2015 — Chinese National Standard for Bibliographic References
regions_primary: China — mandatory for all Chinese-language academic theses
citation_styles.description: Mandatory Chinese national standard for bibliographic references in academic works. Numeric style, ordered by appearance. Required at Tsinghua, Peking University, Fudan, Zhejiang, and all Chinese universities for Chinese-language theses.
citation_styles.notes: Chinese students writing English-language theses at international programs may use IEEE or APA instead.
